exemplar.title: To what extent did the Pradhan Mantri Garib Kalyan Ann Yojana (PM- GKAY) 2020 impact the economic well-being of low-income households of Mysuru in Karnataka, India?

detailedExemplarData.title: To what extent did the Pradhan Mantri Garib Kalyan Ann Yojana (PM- GKAY) 2020 impact the economic well-being of low-income households of Mysuru in Karnataka, India?

subject.title: Economics
subject.description:
**IB Economics** is a **Group 3** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **microeconomics, macroeconomics, the global economy, and development economics**, with a strong focus on **analytical and evaluative skills, real-world application of economic theory, and quantitative analysis**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2024**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Extended response questions | 1h 15min | 1h 30min | 30% |
| **Paper 2** | Data response questions | 1h 45min | 1h 45min | 40% (SL), 30% (HL) |
| **Paper 3** | Mathematical questions | N/A | 1h | 20% (HL only) |
| **Internal Assessment** | Portfolio of 3 commentaries | 20 hours | 20 hours | 20% |

strengths: Research question is clearly stated, narrowly focused and repeatedly linked to the analysis and conclusion.
Topic and purpose are communicated accurately and effectively throughout the report.
Methodology employs an appropriate range of primary and secondary sources with clear evidence of informed selection.
weaknesses: Scope still spans three distinct welfare dimensions, slightly stretching the focus.
Convenience sampling and limited discussion of test assumptions reduce methodological rigour.
Minor redundancies in headings and duplicated titles occasionally disrupt flow.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The essay consistently communicates its topic and purpose with precision, presents a focused and well-bounded research question, and applies a wellâjustified methodology.  While the scope could be narrowed further and robustness checks could be more thoroughly discussed, the overall performance meets the highest band descriptors for Focus and Method.

strengths: Clear and coherent demonstration of economic knowledge with effective integration of relevant theory (e.g. capability approach, externalities).
Accurate and consistent use of specialized economic terminology across the report.
Application of local statistics and a range of academic sources strengthens contextual relevance.
weaknesses: Some sources are name-checked or duplicated rather than critically triangulated.
Triangulation of primary and secondary material could be deeper to avoid descriptive listing.
Occasional conceptual compression around causality limits full depth of understanding.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student shows a strong grasp of economic concepts and applies them coherently to the context, using terminology accurately.  Source material is mostly relevant and wellâintegrated, though critical triangulation and deeper application could be improved.  This performance sits solidly at the upper end of the 3â4 band, meriting 5 out of 6.

strengths: Research is consistently appropriate and directly relevant to the research question, with effective triangulation of primary and secondary data.
Analysis is wellâdeveloped, linking statistical results to hypotheses and economic theory with supported conclusions.
Arguments are logically structured and include clear acknowledgements of key limitations (e.g. sample size, correlation vs. causation).
weaknesses: Robustness checks are limited and causality is occasionally overstated.
Evaluation of secondary data biases and alternative counterarguments is insufficiently critical.
Some factual errors (e.g. misquoted poverty figures) and unclear sampling terminology weaken overall evaluation.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The essay demonstrates a high degree of critical thinking: it selects and interprets research appropriately, offers effective analysis with wellâsupported conclusions, and constructs a coherent argument that acknowledges limitations.  Minor weaknesses in robustness and depth of evaluation place it at the top of the 7â9 band, justifying 9 out of 12.

strengths: Structure follows Extended Essay conventions clearly, with effective headings, numbered pages, and a detailed table of contents.
Layout and formatting are correctly applied: consistent citation style, clear figure labels, and appropriate pagination.
Overall presentation aids readability and navigation across all sections.
weaknesses: Heavy reliance on superscript footnotes disrupts narrative flow; some citation integration could be smoother.
Minor formatting glitches (stray code snippets, inconsistent image sizing) are still present.
Occasional informal phrasing and duplicated headings detract slightly from academic tone.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The reportâs presentation is both clearly structured and wellâformatted, meeting the top band descriptors for structure appropriateness and layout.  While minor formatting and stylistic issues remain, the overall quality of presentation warrants the maximum 4 marks out of 4.

strandSelections.reasoning: The research question is explicitly quoted on the title page and revisited in the conclusion.  It narrows the investigation to one policy, one year and one district, giving clear boundaries and a direct link to subsequent analysis.  While ambitious in covering several welfare dimensions, it remains sufficiently focused for systematic investigation; hence the top band is justified, though a perfect 6 is withheld because the question still spans three outcome areas, slightly stretching focus.

The student introduces the pandemic context, explains the publicâpolicy scheme, and situates it convincingly within the local Mysuru setting.  The purposeâmeasuring impacts on economic well-beingâremains visible throughout sections 1-4 and is appropriate for an Economics EE.  Minor wording slips (e.g. duplicated headings) do not seriously obscure the focus, therefore a strong but not flawless performance merits the upper band.

Primary survey results, government statistics and academic papers are woven through each analytical subsection.  Most citations are pertinent to claims made (food prices, poverty rates, stress reduction).  Nonetheless some sources are merely name-checked or duplicated, and critical triangulation is limited, so the application is âmostlyâ rather than âconsistentlyâ relevant.

Sections follow EE conventions (title page, abstract absent but introduction clear, methodology, analysis, conclusion, bibliography, appendices).  Headings and sub-headings aid navigation, although some duplication and minor mis-numbering appear.  Overall the structure is clearly appropriate but not flawless.

A table of contents, figure labels, word count, and numbered pages are provided.  Citation style is broadly consistent.  Some formatting glitches (stray code, image sizing) are visible, preventing the highest polish but still satisfying the âcorrectly appliedâ descriptor.

The argument progresses logically from hypotheses to conclusion and acknowledges limitations (sample size, correlation â  causation).  Evaluation of savings results is partially critical, but broader counter-arguments, policy shortcomings and alternative explanations receive limited space.  This fits the upper level of the 4-6 band.

Analysis links quantitative results to hypotheses, applies theory, and interprets statistical significance.  The student compares mean shifts, plots expenditure changes and discusses welfare implications.  Yet causality is occasionally overstated and robustness checks are few, so the depth is strong but not exemplary, meriting 7 rather than 9.

Discussion of positive consumption externalities, capability approach, food-security theory and key macro indicators demonstrates clear, coherent economic knowledge.  Concepts are integrated into argumentation rather than listed descriptively, and the student links theory to context (e.g. under-allocation diagram).  Minor conceptual compression (e.g. conflating savings causality) prevents a 6.

The majority of research (survey, state economic surveys, NFHS, IMF papers) directly addresses the RQ, and triangulation between primary and secondary data is evident.  Relevance is high, though the convenience sampling and limited critique of secondary bias keep the work at the lower edge of the 7-9 band.

The student describes primary data collection (56-household questionnaire, t-tests, ÏÂ² test) and a wide body of secondary material.  Source selection shows thought, yet the convenience sample, limited size, and lack of robustness checks reveal only partial methodological rigour.  Statistical outputs are presented but raw data and test assumptions are not fully discussed.  Overall the methods are relevant but not consistently âeffectiveâ, warranting the mid-high band rather than full marks.

Economic terminologyââpaired t-testâ, âmarket failureâ, âpositive externalityâ, âfood insecurityââis used accurately and consistently across 40+ pages.  Occasional typographical noise (Greek letters, stray symbols) does not impede clarity, so the strand meets the highest quality descriptors.

annotations.comment: An opening statement of the specific research gap would sharpen the introductionâs focus.
Heavy reliance on superscript footnotes disrupts narrative flow; integrate key citations into the text.
Footnote formatting for Kumar et al. lacks access date and journal details, reducing reference precision.
Local statistics on Karnataka poverty provide clear contextual relevance and strengthen the analysis.
The title of the investigation is clearly stated, meeting formatting requirements for the title.
The word count is provided prominently and falls within the 4,000-word limit, fulfilling formatting requirements.
The duplicate "Karnataka" headings create redundancy and could be consolidated to improve presentation.
The Table of Contents header is clear and follows extended essay conventions, aiding navigation.
Inconsistent terminology in TOC entries (e.g., trailing period in â2.2 SECONDARY DATA.â) undermines formatting consistency.
Consider including page numbers for appendix entries in the TOC to improve navigation to supplementary materials.
The claim of "23 crore" people slipping below the poverty line is likely erroneous (should be in lakhs); verify the figure to ensure accuracy.
Identical citation text in footnotes 9 and 10 duplicates sources and should be consolidated.
The math formatting for the launch date (2020^{15}) is distracting; use plain text for years.
The Gupta et al. reference in footnote 16 lacks consistent journal formatting; standardize reference style.
Restating the research question verbatim here is redundant; consider cross-referencing the title page instead.
Section 1.1 follows extended essay conventions with clear headings, aiding structure.
First-person phrasing (âMy interest was piquedâ) is informal; revise to a neutral, academic tone.
The scope covers three distinct outcome areas; narrowing to one or two would strengthen focus.
Hypotheses are clearly articulated and directly linked to the research question.
âRandom convenience samplingâ is contradictory; clarify sampling and discuss limitations in bias.
Methodology overview is concise; consider adding a brief note on questionnaire validation.
Explain the assumptions behind t-tests and Chi-Square tests to strengthen methodological rigour.
Secondary data section should note potential biases and time-period alignment with primary data.
The NFSA reference lacks a full URL; ensure all citations are complete and uniformly formatted.
The introduction outlines COVID-19 context but does not explicitly state the research gap; adding this would strengthen focus.
